channel.title: Device-Busy on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Device-Busy on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: ERROR_BUSY (170) - How to Fix
item.description:
ERROR_BUSY (Win32 Error 170)

This Win32 API error occurs when a requested device or resource is busy and cannot be used. The error code is ERROR_BUSY (value 170). The full message reads:

“The device or resource is busy.”

This commonly appears when trying to access a device, file, or port that is exclusively held by another process.

Common Causes

  • Device in use — Another application has exclusive access to the device.
  • File locked — File is being used by another process.
  • Port occupied — Serial/parallel port already opened.
  • Volume locked — Disk volume exclusively mounted.
  • Service conflict — Another service is using the resource.

How to Fix

Find Process Using the Resource

handle "device_name_or_path"

Or using PowerShell:

item.title: [Solution] Linux EBUSY (errno 16) — Device or Resource Busy Fix
item.description:
Linux EBUSY (errno 16) — Device or Resource Busy

EBUSY (errno 16) means you tried to use a resource that is currently locked or in use by another process. This error appears when attempting to unmount a filesystem, remove a disk, delete a file held open by a running program, or modify a device that is actively being written to. On servers, EBUSY is a frequent obstacle during deployments, log rotation, and disk maintenance.
